g.title: projects
g.text: A portfolio project — the unit that groups repositories and can consume microservice projects.
Added v12, default 'standard'. No SQL CHECK — the legal vocabulary is enforced in Rust only.
Manual ordering (v14); back-filled as id * 10 to leave gaps for inserts.
v28 — 0/1 opt-in for the background auto-sync timer.

g.title: settings
g.text: Flat key/value application settings. Never holds secrets — the GitHub PAT and the encryption data key live in the OS credential store.

g.title: templates
g.text: Deploy/scaffold template files per language key, including each stack's meta.json (v25 reads it to decide which placeholders are repo-scope).
0 = seeded from the bundled templates, 1 = edited by the user (never overwritten by re-seeding).

g.title: project_microservices
g.text: Many-to-many link between a parent project and a consumed microservice project. Rebuilt in v12 — before that it linked a project to a repository. Composite PK plus CHECK(project_id != microservice_project_id), so a project cannot consume itself.

g.title: project_microservices:microservice_project_id->projects:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (microservice_project_id) REFERENCES projects (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: project_microservices:project_id->projects:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: repositories
g.text: A repository belonging to at most one project. May be GitHub-backed or local-only.
ON DELETE SET NULL — the only non-cascading FK in the schema. Deleting a project orphans its repositories rather than destroying them.
UNIQUE, and nullable since v13 so local-only repos (never pushed to GitHub) can exist. The last path segment is the canonical folder name used for all cross-repo doc folders.
'microservice' is still legal here but is legacy: v12 NULLed every such row when microservice became a project type rather than a repo role.
Stable GitHub identifier — the basis of rename detection.
Lazy-migration marker (v18); NULL means the repo's MD has never been imported.
Lazy-migration marker (v21). While NULL, the first task sync suppresses 'created' events so backfilling legacy files doesn't flood the timeline.
v25 — JSON map of repo-wide (not per-environment) deploy placeholder values.
v28 — NULL means auto-commit is off for this repo.

g.title: repositories:project_id->projects:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE SET NULL MATCH NONE

g.title: repo_renames
g.text: Append-only log of repository renames, written when a known github_id shows up under a new github_name. The sync preamble replays every entry to rename cross-repo folders on the counterparty's disk. There is deliberately no applied_at column — idempotency comes from filesystem checks, not DB state.

g.title: repo_renames:repository_id->repositories:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (repository_id) REFERENCES repositories (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: bugs
g.text: Source of truth for bug reports. docs/bug-reports.md in each repo is a generated, LLM-facing view of this table — protected fields are restored from here on every reconcile.
Per-repository counter; display_id is its rendered form (B-000042).
App-managed: incremented on each transition into 'testing'. Never written by an AI agent — this is what makes the attempt history honest.
v22 — set when the agent removes a confirmed row from the MD file. After that, regeneration permanently excludes the row.

g.title: bugs:repository_id->repositories:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (repository_id) REFERENCES repositories (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: bug_events
g.text: Append-only bug lifecycle log (v19), so attempts-per-period metrics are computed from events rather than a running counter. Invariant: the count of 'entered_testing' events for a bug equals bugs.fix_attempts.

g.title: bug_events:bug_id->bugs:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (bug_id) REFERENCES bugs (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: deploy_environments
g.text: One deploy environment (prod / staging / any custom name) for a repository; 1:N per repo since v20, which replaced the single deploy_manifests row.
JSON map of environment-scope placeholder values. Repo-wide ones live in repositories.deploy_repo_config.

g.title: deploy_environments:repository_id->repositories:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (repository_id) REFERENCES repositories (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: deploy_secrets
g.text: Per-environment per-secret flags: which role the secret plays and whether it is included or overridden. Values are NOT stored here.
build / deploy / runtime — decides where the secret is injected.
0 excludes the secret from this environment without deleting its configuration.

g.title: deploy_secrets:deploy_env_id->deploy_environments:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (deploy_env_id) REFERENCES deploy_environments (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: tasks
g.text: Mirror of docs/todo.md and docs/done.md. Polarity is the reverse of bugs — here the Markdown is canonical and this table is rebuilt from it on each sync, because tasks are hand-edited far more often than bugs.
The display id as written in Markdown (T-000042 / F-000013 / D-000001) — TEXT, not a foreign key. Note task_events.task_id is a different thing: an INTEGER FK to tasks.id.
No SQL CHECK — critical / high / medium / low are enforced in Rust.
No SQL CHECK — open / in-progress / review are enforced in Rust.
Inherited from the nearest preceding '## vX.Y.Z' header in todo.md.
'todo' or 'done'. Part of the uniqueness key, so the same id may exist once per source during a transition.

g.title: tasks:repository_id->repositories:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (repository_id) REFERENCES repositories (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: task_events
g.text: Append-only task lifecycle log, the task-side twin of bug_events.
INTEGER FK to tasks.id — not the display id (that is tasks.task_id, TEXT).

g.title: task_events:task_id->tasks:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (task_id) REFERENCES tasks (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: deploy_events
g.text: Log of deploy operations (template render, GitHub secret set/delete). deploy_env_id is nullable so repo-level actions can be recorded too.

g.title: deploy_events:repository_id->repositories:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (repository_id) REFERENCES repositories (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: deploy_events:deploy_env_id->deploy_environments:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (deploy_env_id) REFERENCES deploy_environments (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: project_renames
g.text: Project-level twin of repo_renames (v24). Needed because microservice-api/<name>/ folders on the parent-server side are keyed by project name, which repo_renames does not cover.

g.title: project_renames:project_id->projects:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: sync_events
g.text: Log of sync operations, feeding the timeline. repository_id is nullable — a NULL row is a portfolio-wide sync rather than a per-repo one.
project_sync / tasks / secret / requirements / migration. 'migration' was added in v25 to record deploy-config conflicts resolved by first-env-wins.

g.title: sync_events:repository_id->repositories:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (repository_id) REFERENCES repositories (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: secret_bundles
g.text: v26 — a named, reusable set of secret values (the SSH / DB / npm keys you apply across repos). Portfolio-wide: deliberately not tied to a repository.

g.title: secret_bundle_items
g.text: One secret inside a bundle, encrypted with the same key and cipher as deploy_secret_values.

g.title: secret_bundle_items:bundle_id->secret_bundles:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (bundle_id) REFERENCES secret_bundles (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE

g.title: deploy_secret_values
g.text: v27 — persisted deploy secret values, encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM. The data key lives in the OS keyring; no plaintext value is ever stored.
AES-256-GCM ciphertext. Never logged, never exported.
Per-value random nonce; required to decrypt ciphertext.

g.title: deploy_secret_values:deploy_env_id->deploy_environments:id
g.text: FOREIGN KEY (deploy_env_id) REFERENCES deploy_environments (id) ON UPDATE NO ACTION ON DELETE CASCADE MATCH NONE
